brand.name: SolForge Fortune
path.title: Continue with these resources
footer.disclaimer: Entertainment and cultural material based on traditional symbols. It does not predict the future or replace health, financial or legal decisions.
daily.title: One-line daily notes for the 12 animals
daily.note: Prewritten notes rotate by date and animal sign. They do not analyze a person or predict events and are intended only for light reflection.
dynamic.theme.0: Choose one thing that truly needs finishing today instead of rushing through everything.
dynamic.theme.1: Add one small change to a familiar method; a different angle may loosen a stuck task.
dynamic.theme.2: Check the other person's intent before responding to reduce an avoidable misunderstanding.
dynamic.theme.3: Use your energy on the most important task and move the rest into a clear next order.
dynamic.theme.4: Write down what is already working; the next direction may become clearer than the remaining gaps.
dynamic.theme.5: Keeping a small promise and respecting time can strengthen trust today.
dynamic.theme.6: Pause to organize your surroundings and your thoughts may become easier to sort as well.
dynamic.theme.7: Ask once more for the information you need instead of reaching a conclusion alone.
dynamic.theme.8: There may be more satisfaction in finishing an existing task than adding another new one.
dynamic.theme.9: Express thanks for a small kindness you usually pass over and the tone of a relationship may soften.
dynamic.theme.10: It is fine if reality moves more slowly than the plan; reset a pace you can sustain.
dynamic.theme.11: Sort today's decision slowly around verified facts rather than the strongest passing emotion.
pages.home.meta.title: Daily Animal Fortunes and Symbol Guides | SolForge Fortune
pages.home.meta.description: Read daily notes for the 12 animals without entering personal data, then explore cultural guides to animal signs, constellations and dream symbols.
pages.home.hero.title: Short prompts for reflecting on the day, not predictions that decide it.
pages.home.hero.body: Read one-line notes for the 12 animals and prepared guides to animal signs, constellations and dream symbols without entering a birth date or personal information.
pages.home.hero.noteBody: Fortunes are entertainment and cultural reflection. Never delegate financial, medical, legal or safety decisions to a fortune.
pages.home.reading.title: Four ways to read fortunes lightly and safely
pages.home.reading.intro: Treat a note as a question about thoughts and actions, not a fixed future.
pages.home.sections.tradition.title: Fortunes are symbol traditions that vary by place and time
pages.home.sections.tradition.body: Animal signs, constellations and auspicious readings developed in different cultures and eras. The same symbol can carry different meanings and has no single definitive interpretation.
pages.home.sections.rhythm.title: A daily note does not direct your actions
pages.home.sections.rhythm.body: Use it to reflect on pace, priorities and feelings. Real schedules, promises and verified facts always take priority over the note.
pages.home.sections.relationships.title: Relationship fortunes do not reveal another person's mind
pages.home.sections.relationships.body: Do not infer someone's thoughts or actions from a sign. Direct conversation and consent matter; use the note only to reconsider how you listen and speak.
pages.home.sections.reflection.title: Remember that broadly applicable lines can feel uniquely accurate
pages.home.sections.reflection.body: Let irrelevant text pass. Important decisions require objective information and qualified help rather than a memorable match.
pages.dailyGuide.meta.title: How to Read a Daily Fortune | SolForge Fortune
pages.dailyGuide.meta.description: Use six reflection prompts for overall priorities, focus, relationships, routine, caution and review.
pages.dailyGuide.hero.title: Turn a daily fortune into six questions for reflection
pages.dailyGuide.hero.body: Instead of a good-or-bad verdict, use open questions about priorities, emotions, communication and daily rhythm.
pages.dailyGuide.hero.noteBody: Investments, contracts, treatment, medication, legal responses and safety require current facts and qualified professional guidance.
pages.dailyGuide.reading.title: Six questions for reviewing the day
pages.dailyGuide.reading.intro: Convert a fortune line into an open question that fits the real situation.
pages.dailyGuide.sections.overall.title: Overall: what is the one essential task today?
pages.dailyGuide.sections.overall.body: Separate what must be handled from what can wait. Your schedule and responsibilities take priority over the tone of a fortune.
pages.dailyGuide.sections.focus.title: Focus: where should limited energy go?
pages.dailyGuide.sections.focus.body: If attention is scattered, work on one task in short blocks. Check what is already underway before adding something new.
pages.dailyGuide.sections.relationship.t: Relationships: are you assuming without checking?
pages.dailyGuide.sections.relationship.b: Do not judge intent or emotion from a sign. Ask, listen and record important agreements in clear words.
pages.dailyGuide.sections.routine.title: Routine: are the basics being maintained?
pages.dailyGuide.sections.routine.body: Review sleep, meals, rest and order. Persistent symptoms or anxiety require medical or professional support, not fortune interpretation.
pages.dailyGuide.sections.caution.title: Caution: does the message amplify fear?
pages.dailyGuide.sections.caution.body: Be wary of claims that guarantee disaster, accidents or separation or demand payment. Stop when fear is used to sell charms or consultations.
pages.dailyGuide.sections.reflection.bod: At day's end, record choices and outcomes rather than whether the fortune was right. That is more useful for planning tomorrow.
pages.zodiac.meta.title: Symbols and Traits of the 12 Animal Signs | SolForge Fortune
pages.zodiac.meta.description: A balanced cultural guide to symbols associated with the Rat through Pig signs.
pages.zodiac.hero.title: The 12 animals are inherited symbols, not personality verdicts
pages.zodiac.hero.body: Explore animal images connected with birth years as cultural stories. People with one sign still differ through environment, experience and choice.
pages.zodiac.hero.noteBody: Animal-year boundaries may not match January 1. Confirm whether a system uses Lunar New Year, Start of Spring or another convention.
pages.zodiac.reading.title: Representative images associated with the 12 animals
pages.zodiac.reading.intro: Each apparent strength can become a burden in another context, so both sides are included.
pages.zodiac.sections.rat.title: Rat: agility and preparation
pages.zodiac.sections.rat.body: Linked with noticing small opportunities and gathering resources. Use it as a prompt to organize information and act step by step rather than overcalculate.
pages.zodiac.sections.ox.title: Ox: consistency and responsibility
pages.zodiac.sections.ox.body: Represents continuing a task even at a slower pace. Balance persistence with checkpoints that allow direction to change.
pages.zodiac.sections.tiger.title: Tiger: courage and momentum
pages.zodiac.sections.tiger.body: Associated with leading and deciding. Distinguish moments for quick action from moments that need more voices.
pages.zodiac.sections.rabbit.title: Rabbit: sensitivity and harmony
pages.zodiac.sections.rabbit.body: An image of reading the room and softening conflict. Also ask whether harmony repeatedly pushes your own needs aside.
pages.zodiac.sections.dragon.title: Dragon: vision and expansion
pages.zodiac.sections.dragon.body: Symbolizes large aims and transformative energy. Pair ambition with a realistic schedule and a concrete collaboration process.
pages.zodiac.sections.snake.title: Snake: observation and insight
pages.zodiac.sections.snake.body: Linked with quiet observation and finding the core. After enough thought, share what others need to know clearly.
pages.zodiac.sections.horse.title: Horse: activity and independence
pages.zodiac.sections.horse.body: Represents movement, freedom and new experiences. Before accelerating, confirm the destination and the people traveling with you.
pages.zodiac.sections.goat.title: Goat: care and creativity
pages.zodiac.sections.goat.body: An image of warm collaboration and delicate expression. Protect your time and boundaries as carefully as you care for others.
pages.zodiac.sections.monkey.title: Monkey: wit and adaptation
pages.zodiac.sections.monkey.body: Symbolizes adjusting quickly and finding alternatives. After generating ideas, choose one and carry it to completion.
pages.zodiac.sections.rooster.title: Rooster: precision and expression
pages.zodiac.sections.rooster.body: Linked with clear standards, time and speech. Consider order and context so precision is not heard only as criticism.
pages.zodiac.sections.dog.title: Dog: trust and protection
pages.zodiac.sections.dog.body: Represents loyalty and sensitivity to unfairness. Asking for help instead of carrying every duty alone is also part of trust.
pages.zodiac.sections.pig.title: Pig: abundance and openness
pages.zodiac.sections.pig.body: Associated with generosity and an unguarded manner. Pair willingness to share with limits on resources and promises.
pages.constellations.meta.title: The 12 Constellations and Four Elements | SolForge Fortune
pages.constellations.meta.description: A cultural guide to the fire, earth, air and water groups, date boundaries and safe interpretation.
pages.constellations.hero.title: Read constellations lightly as a symbolic language of four elements
pages.constellations.hero.body: Popular Western astrology describes traits from the Sun sign. It is presented as cultural symbolism, not a scientific personality test or forecasting tool.
pages.constellations.hero.noteBody: Transition dates can vary by year, time zone and system. A simple date table cannot settle every boundary birth.
pages.constellations.reading.title: The four elements and their sign groups
pages.constellations.reading.intro: Each group is a metaphor containing both strengths and balancing questions.
pages.constellations.sections.fire.title: Fire: Aries, Leo and Sagittarius
pages.constellations.sections.fire.body: Images of beginnings, enthusiasm and expression. Add a question about coordinating with people who move at a different pace.
pages.constellations.sections.earth.body: Images of practicality, senses and structure. Value consistency while checking for signals that a familiar method should change.
pages.constellations.sections.air.title: Air: Gemini, Libra and Aquarius
pages.constellations.sections.air.body: Images of thought, conversation and connection. After gathering perspectives, check whether action and emotion were included.
pages.constellations.sections.water.body: Images of feeling, intuition and empathy. Protect boundaries and recovery time while attending to the emotional climate.
pages.constellations.sections.boundaries: Popular horoscopes usually use only the Sun sign. Astronomical constellations and astrological divisions differ, and systems using birth time and place also vary.
pages.constellations.sections.reading.ti: Use questions instead of personality labels
pages.constellations.sections.reading.bo: Do not fix behavior with 'I am this sign.' Use the metaphor to consider an attitude that helps now and a habit you want to change.
pages.dreamSymbols.meta.title: A Guide to Common Dream Symbols | SolForge Fortune
pages.dreamSymbols.meta.description: Explore water, fire, animals, journeys, homes, falling and finding with respect for personal context.
pages.dreamSymbols.hero.title: Start with the emotion and experience, not a fixed symbol dictionary
pages.dreamSymbols.hero.body: The same water or animal carries different memories for different people. Use dreams as records of recent experience, not announcements of future events.
pages.dreamSymbols.hero.noteBody: If recurring nightmares or sleep problems disrupt life, seek medical, sleep or mental-health support rather than relying on interpretation.
pages.dreamSymbols.reading.title: Eight recurring types of dream imagery
pages.dreamSymbols.reading.intro: These are context questions, not universal meanings.
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.water.title: Water: an image for emotional state
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.water.body: Record whether the scene felt calm or frightening before labeling clarity or depth. Ask whether recent feelings had enough room for expression.
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.fire.title: Fire: an image of change and tension
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.fire.body: A warm fire and an uncontrolled fire feel different. Consider whether recent life feels closer to vitality or overload.
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.animals.body: Separate personal experience from cultural symbolism. Whether the animal attacked, protected or accompanied you may be more useful context.
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.journey.titl: Roads and travel: choice and transition
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.journey.body: Record destination, being lost and companions. The scene can prompt reflection on hopes and burdens around a real transition.
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.home.title: Homes and rooms: familiarity and private space
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.home.body: Note what differed from a real home and what felt open or closed. It can prompt thoughts about rest, boundaries and unfinished matters.
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.falling.titl: Falling and lateness: scenes about control
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.falling.body: Do not treat them as warnings of failure. Consider recent pressure, preparation concerns and physical sensations during sleep.
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.finding.titl: Finding and losing: value and priority
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.finding.body: Record what was lost or found and its personal meaning. Reflect on what matters now and what feels neglected.
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.context.titl: Context records matter more than symbol lists
pages.dreamSymbols.sections.context.body: Keep date, scene, emotion, recent events and sleep state together. Do not turn one searched word into luck, danger or number recommendations.
pages.culture.meta.title: Understanding Animal Signs, Constellations and Auspicious-Day Culture | SolForge Fortune
pages.culture.meta.description: Differences among animal signs, Western astrology and traditional almanac customs, with respectful modern use.
pages.culture.hero.title: Do not blend different fortune traditions into one rule
pages.culture.hero.body: East Asian animal and calendar traditions and Western astrology have different histories and calculations. Distinguishing background reduces exaggerated claims.
pages.culture.hero.noteBody: Do not mock tradition as mere superstition or impose it as scientific fact. Respect community meaning while prioritizing personal choice.
pages.culture.reading.title: Six perspectives for understanding fortune traditions
pages.culture.reading.intro: Separate origin, purpose, modern use and limitations.
pages.culture.sections.twelve.title: The 12 animals and cyclical signs
pages.culture.sections.twelve.body: The animals connect with a cycle used for years, months, days and hours. Birth-year animals are common in daily life, but boundaries and interpretations differ by system.
pages.culture.sections.western.title: Western constellations and astrology
pages.culture.sections.western.body: Popular horoscopes usually use the Sun sign, while traditional astrology can use birth time and location. Its purpose differs from scientific astronomy.
pages.culture.sections.almanac.title: Traditional almanacs and auspicious dates
pages.culture.sections.almanac.body: Some customs consult dates for moving or weddings. Consent, cost, weather and administration take priority; tradition remains an optional reference.
pages.culture.sections.differences.title: Differences among systems
pages.culture.sections.differences.body: Animal signs, Four Pillars, constellations, tarot and dream interpretation use different symbols. Conflicting results do not mean one objectively fixes the future.
pages.culture.sections.modern.title: Modern use
pages.culture.sections.modern.body: Enjoy them as conversation, ceremony and reflection. Do not use them to screen job applicants, partners or other people.
pages.culture.sections.respect.title: Respect choice and consent
pages.culture.sections.respect.body: Do not demand birth details or impose a reading on someone who declines. Respect privacy and religious and cultural perspectives.
pages.methodology.meta.title: Fortune Content Methodology | SolForge Fortune
pages.methodology.meta.description: How daily notes rotate, which date is used, cultural scope, prediction limits and safety principles.
pages.methodology.hero.title: We explain exactly how today's note is selected
pages.methodology.hero.body: Daily animal notes rotate deterministically through prewritten lines using the local date and animal order. No personal profile or predictive model is used.
pages.methodology.hero.noteTitle: The same result on the same date
pages.methodology.hero.noteBody: Every visitor receives the same line for one animal on the same local date. It does not indicate knowledge of a person or event.
pages.methodology.reading.title: Six standards for fortune material
pages.methodology.reading.intro: Preserve entertainment and cultural context while reducing fear and decision risk.
pages.methodology.sections.rotation.body: Twelve lines about pace, conversation, order, rest and priorities rotate by date and animal. The process does not analyze individuals.
pages.methodology.sections.date.title: Date and time zone
pages.methodology.sections.date.body: The visitor's local device date determines the note, so changeover differs across time zones. No birth date is sent to a server.
pages.methodology.sections.sources.title: Scope of cultural guides
pages.methodology.sections.sources.body: Widely known animal and four-element symbols are introduced without declaring one orthodox meaning. Interpretations vary by history, region and transmission.
pages.methodology.sections.prediction.bo: We do not claim to predict accidents, illness, pregnancy, exams, separation or returns, and recommend no numbers, stocks, lottery entries or treatments.
pages.methodology.sections.safety.title: Safety principles
pages.methodology.sections.safety.body: We avoid fear and payment pressure. Health, safety, financial and legal issues point readers to official information and qualified help.
pages.methodology.sections.disclaimer.bo: Cultural errors are corrected after review. All content is entertainment and education and does not replace important personal decisions.
pages.about.meta.title: About | SolForge Fortune
pages.about.meta.description: The mission, coverage, bilingual operation and editorial independence of SolForge Fortune.
pages.about.hero.title: A light fortune and symbol library with no personal data entry
pages.about.hero.body: Daily notes for the 12 animals sit alongside cultural guides designed not to fix the future or exploit anxiety.
pages.about.hero.noteBody: We explain cultural background, reading methods and limits instead of repeating short results alone.
pages.about.reading.title: What the site provides
pages.about.reading.intro: Daily notes, symbol guides, cultural context and safe-use principles share one navigation structure.
pages.about.sections.mission.title: Mission
pages.about.sections.mission.body: Make fortunes enjoyable as light cultural reflection rather than frightening predictions.
pages.about.sections.scope.title: Coverage
pages.about.sections.scope.body: The 12 animals, four constellation elements, recurring dream imagery and differences among fortune traditions.
pages.about.sections.bilingual.title: Korean and English
pages.about.sections.bilingual.body: Core pages share the same structure in both languages, and the language switch preserves the current path.
pages.about.sections.independence.title: Editorial independence
pages.about.sections.independence.body: We do not sell readings, charms or divination products or claim that one service is uniquely accurate.
pages.privacy.meta.title: Privacy | SolForge Fortune
pages.privacy.meta.description: How SolForge Fortune handles user input, browser processing, hosting logs and potential advertising.
pages.privacy.hero.title: Daily material without requiring birth details
pages.privacy.hero.body: The independent fortune site currently works without account, name, birth date or contact input.
pages.privacy.hero.noteBody: Never enter a government ID, precise birth time or place, payment data or account password here. SolForge does not request them.
pages.privacy.reading.title: Current processing scope
pages.privacy.reading.intro: This policy will be updated to match actual behavior if features or advertising change.
pages.privacy.sections.inputs.title: User input
pages.privacy.sections.inputs.body: Daily animal notes display prewritten lines without collecting a visitor's name or birth information.
pages.privacy.sections.storage.title: Browser processing
pages.privacy.sections.storage.body: The browser reads the device's current date and does not store a personal fortune history or profile on the server.
pages.privacy.sections.hosting.title: Hosting and access logs
pages.privacy.sections.hosting.body: Cloudflare Pages delivers content and may process basic logs for security, reliability and statistics under Cloudflare policy.
pages.privacy.sections.advertising.title: Advertising and cookies
pages.privacy.sections.advertising.body: No advertising script is included at launch. Future ads will not cover main content and will include required notice and consent.
pages.privacy.sections.contact.title: Contact and changes
pages.privacy.sections.contact.body: Privacy questions can be submitted through the main SolForge contact page. Processing changes will also update this page.
